articles.title: Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi declines amid rising crude oil prices, escalating US-Iran war tensions
articles.description: Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi fell 0.74% at the opening bell, while the small-cap Kosdaq index declined 1.66%.

articles.title: Gold slips as oil prices advance, Fed rate-hike voices grow
articles.description: Gold prices dropped on Monday as Middle East tensions escalated significantly. Brent crude oil surpassed ninety dollars a barrel, increasing inflation worries. Several U.S. Federal Reserve officials indicated potential interest rate hikes are necessary. Asian share markets showed hesitation ahead of major technology company earnings reports. Consumer sentiment saw a temporary boost, but rising oil prices may reverse this trend.

articles.title: Gift Nifty, US-Iran war, oil prices to Q1 results: 8 key things that changed for Indian stock market over weekend
articles.description: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,293 level, a discount of nearly 29 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a weak start for the Indian stock market indices.

articles.title: Brent crude oil tops $90 as US, Iran intensify attacks
articles.description: Oil prices jumped 3% on Monday, with Brent ​surpassing $90 a barrel, as the United States and Iran ‌expanded attacks in the Middle East that have ​curbed energy shipments in the Strait of ⁠Hormuz.

articles.title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90
articles.description: The dollar strengthened against global peers as Middle East tensions escalated. Oil prices surged and investor confidence remained fragile after last week's market turbulence. Geopolitical concerns and semiconductor valuation worries weighed on market sentiment. Federal Reserve officials are debating potential interest rate increases to combat inflation. Cryptocurrencies like bitcoin and ether saw modest gains in early trading.

articles.title: Brent oil tops $90 as US, Iran intensify attacks in Middle East
articles.description: Oil prices climbed significantly on Monday as tensions rose. Brent crude futures surpassed ninety dollars a barrel, reaching recent highs. Attacks in the Middle East have disrupted energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. This escalation follows a substantial weekly gain for oil prices. Analysts suggest markets are underestimating potential inventory impacts from these blockades.

articles.title: Gold slips as oil prices advance, Fed rate-hike voices grow
articles.description: Gold prices fell on Monday as an escalation in the Middle East war pushed Brent crude above $90 a barrel, heightening inflation concerns as several ​U.S. Federal Reserve policymakers signalled that interest rate hikes may be ‌needed to curb price pressures.

articles.title: Trading Plan: Can Nifty 50 hold above 24,300 and Bank Nifty defend 58,500 amid Middle East war-driven surge in oil prices?
articles.description: Although momentum indicators have improved, the weekly charts suggest that the Nifty 50 is still trading within a broader range. The index needs to reclaim and sustain above the 24,400–24,500 resistance zone to trigger a further rally. Until then, it may continue to consolidate, with immediate support at 24,200, followed by the 24,000–23,950 zone as the next support area.

articles.title: India FX traders eye oil prices, RBI defence; bonds bulls await index decision
articles.description: The Indian rupee's trajectory this week will hinge on crude oil prices and the extent to which the central bank defends the currency as it nears record-low levels, while bond traders await a decision about inclusion in a global ​debt index.

articles.title: Crude oil prices jump as US-Iran war escalates; Brent oil tops $90 per barrel
articles.description: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures rose $2.69, or 4%, to $90.79 a barrel, their highest level since June 11.
